data.title: Rwa Wealth Partners CIO Comments on Unprecedented Opposition
data.description: On April 30, Jin10 reported that JP Powers, Chief Investment Officer at Rwa Wealth Partners, remarked on the unusual level of opposition seen for the first time in over 30 years, calling it a 'crazy time.' According to Jin10, Powers noted that Milan is somewhat unconventional, advocating for further interest rate cuts at this moment. He expressed certainty that reaching any consensus within the committee would be challenging, especially with oil prices back above $100. Additionally, the transition involving Powell adds difficulty to advancing such measures. Interestingly, Powers highlighted that three other committee members were unwilling to maintain a dovish stance in their statement, which he believes is the crux of the matter.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's Confirmation Hearing Highlights Internal Fed Conflict
data.description: Nick Timiraos has suggested that four dissenting votes within the Federal Reserve may bolster Kevin Warsh's assertion of internal discord. According to NS3.AI, this observation was made in relation to Warsh's confirmation hearing held last week.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Comments on Duration of Ukraine and Iran Conflicts
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump remarked that the conflicts in Ukraine and Iran have persisted for comparable lengths of time. According to NS3.AI, he suggested that these wars might reach their conclusions simultaneously.

data.title: Expert: Market Shows Limited Reaction to Federal Reserve Decisions
data.description: Tony Welch, Chief Investment Officer at Atlanta's SignatureFD, stated that the market has shown limited reaction to the Federal Reserve's decisions, as signals had been released in advance. According to Jin10, Welch noted that the statement from the Fed was more hawkish, with inflation pressures evident even before the energy turmoil. He attributed this partly to the economy's continued unexpected growth, indicating that the environment does not urgently require rate cuts. The bond market has been aware of this for weeks, with interest rates trending upward as the probability of rate cuts slowly decreases. Welch believes that the threshold for rate hikes remains high unless there is some form of economic deterioration.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell Expects March PCE Inflation Rate at 3.5%
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has projected that the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation rate for March will be 3.5%. According to Jin10, Powell's statement reflects ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures in the U.S. economy. The PCE inflation rate is a key indicator used by the Federal Reserve to gauge price changes in consumer goods and services, and it plays a significant role in shaping monetary policy decisions. Powell's forecast suggests that inflation remains a critical issue for policymakers as they navigate economic recovery and stability.

data.title: Oliver Harris Returns to JPMorgan to Lead Kinexys Division
data.description: Oliver Harris has rejoined JPMorgan to head its Kinexys division, emphasizing a cautious approach to tokenization. According to NS3.AI, Harris expressed in a LinkedIn post on Tuesday that Kinexys will concentrate on developing digital settlement infrastructure, enhancing tokenization capabilities, and fostering partnerships across both public and private blockchain networks.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: This is my last press conference as chairman. Congratulations to Warsh.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: This is my last press conference as chairman. Congratulations, Warsh.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: Inflation expectations have risen recently, but the unemployment rate has not changed much.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: Inflation expectations have risen recently, while the unemployment rate has remained largely unchanged. Long-term inflation expectations are consistent with the 2% target. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst Anstey Discusses Divergent Views on Interest Rate Decisions
data.description:
Analyst Anstey has highlighted the complexity of the current economic landscape, suggesting that more time is needed to fully understand the situation. According to Odaily, there is a divergence of opinion among policymakers regarding interest rate decisions. Milan is the sole member advocating for a 0.25 percentage point rate cut, while three other members, Hamark, Kashkari, and Logan, believe maintaining the current rate is appropriate.

Interestingly, these three members, who disagree with the policy inclination, interpret the statement as leaning towards easing. The statement itself is neutral, indicating that the committee will adjust its monetary policy stance as necessary based on actual conditions to mitigate risks that could hinder the committee's objectives. These objectives include maintaining price stability and achieving full employment. However, it appears that the three members perceive the statement as primarily addressing the employment mandate.

data.title: US President Trump: Iran's economy is collapsing, and the Iranian currency is worthless.
data.description: US President Trump: Iran's economy is collapsing. The Iranian currency is worthless, and inflation is rampant. (Jinshi)

data.title: JPMorgan Highlights Federal Reserve's Focus on Price Levels
data.description: JPMorgan has noted that the U.S. Federal Reserve is increasingly concentrating on price levels. According to ChainCatcher, this shift in focus comes amid ongoing economic evaluations and policy considerations by the central bank.

data.title: OpenAI Unveils Five-Point Plan to Enhance Cybersecurity
data.description: OpenAI has announced a new five-point action plan aimed at strengthening cybersecurity measures. According to Jin10, the initiative focuses on improving network defenses to address growing concerns over digital security threats. The plan includes strategies for enhancing encryption protocols, increasing collaboration with industry partners, and implementing advanced AI-driven security solutions. OpenAI's efforts reflect a commitment to safeguarding digital infrastructure in an increasingly interconnected world.

data.title: In the past hour, a total of $182 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, with $64.78 million liquidated in ETH alone.
data.description: Following the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, major cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin (BTC) fell. Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, $182 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, with $177 million in long positions and $5.1811 million in short positions. Specifically, $64.78 million in ETH and $63.64 million in BTC were liquidated.

data.title: The WLFI token unlocking governance proposal is now open for a 7-day voting period.
data.description: World Liberty Financial has initiated a governance proposal vote on token unlocking, involving 62,282,252,205 locked WLFI tokens. According to the proposal, if passed, these tokens will not enter the market for at least two years. The proposal indicates that up to 45.2 billion WLFI held by the founding team, advisors, and partners will be converted to a 2-year lock followed by a 3-year linear unlock, accompanied by the burning of up to approximately 4.5 billion tokens; approximately 17 billion locked tokens held by early supporters are proposed to be converted to a 2-year lock followed by a 2-year linear unlock. The voting period for this proposal is 7 days, with a quorum threshold of 1 billion WLFI.

data.title: Four dissenting votes deepened the rift within the Federal Reserve as Powell stepped down.
data.description: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged as expected, with 12 voting members participating. Four votes were against the change, the highest number since October 1992. According to the FOMC statement, Governor Milan voted against maintaining the rate and supported a 25 basis point cut; Cleveland Fed President Hamack, Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari, and Dallas Fed President Logan voted to maintain the rate but opposed retaining dovish language in the statement. The FOMC statement retained the word "further" in "considering the extent and timing of further adjustments to the target range for the federal funds rate." Investment banks expect the word "further" to be removed to weaken the indication of a rate cut. This meeting is likely Powell's last as Fed Chairman. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise After Fed Holds Rates Steady
data.description: On April 30, U.S. Treasury yields increased following the Federal Reserve's decision to maintain interest rates. According to Jin10, the Fed's decision, marked by the most significant division since 1992, highlighted growing market concerns over inflation. The policy statement faced opposition from three officials who argued against signaling a tendency to lower rates. The two-year Treasury yield, which typically aligns with Fed rate expectations, rose by 7.8 basis points to 3.92%, the highest level since March 27.

data.title: Federal Reserve Describes Inflation as 'Elevated'
data.description: The Federal Reserve has characterized inflation as 'elevated,' a shift from its previous description of being 'slightly high.' According to Jin10, this change in language reflects the impact of global energy prices on inflationary pressures.

data.title: Federal Reserve Rate Decision Faces Most Dissent Since 1992
data.description: The Federal Reserve's recent interest rate decision and policy statement have encountered the highest level of dissent since October 1992. According to Odaily, this marks a significant moment in the history of the Federal Reserve's decision-making process.

data.title: Following the announcement of the Federal Reserve's interest rate policy, short-term US interest rate futures continue to reflect a low probability of a rate cut this year.
data.description: Following the Federal Reserve's decision to keep policy rates unchanged, short-term US interest rate futures continue to reflect a low probability of a rate cut this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: Senator Tillis Pushes for Clarity Act Progress Amid Banking Concerns
data.description: Senator Thom Tillis has announced that progress on the Clarity Act has alleviated several banking-related issues. According to NS3.AI, Tillis plans to encourage the Senate Banking Committee chair to advance the bill to markup. This step could pave the way for a hearing in mid-May, a necessary hurdle before the legislation can proceed to a full Senate vote. Stablecoin rewards continue to be a key point of negotiation, and any further delays could jeopardize the bill's progression through the remaining Senate schedule.
